title: Fragrant Thai Tom-Yum
description: A refreshing, energizing Thai dish featuring a balance of spicy and sour flavors, crunchy sesame seeds, and paleo-friendly ingredients. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large clay pot, heat the sesame seeds over medium heat.
Add the infuse-oil, lemongrass, and galangal to the pot, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chilies, fish sauce, and coconut milk to the pot, and bring to a simmer.
Add the mushrooms, tomatoes, and fish balls (if using) to the pot, and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with lime juice and fish sauce.
Serve the Tom-Yum hot, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and a drizzle of infuse-oil.
If desired, add 2-3 cups of water to the pot, and cook for an additional 5-7 minutes to create a light broth.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and discard the solids.
Reserve 1/4 cup of the broth for serving, and discard the rest.
Serve the Tom-Yum hot, with the reserved broth spooned over the top.
Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves, and serve immediately.
